claim: Bash and file tools are more critical infrastructure for AI agents than the flashy computer use tool because engineers do their best work in terminals and editors
actionable: Prioritize integrating bash terminal and text editor tools into AI agents alongside or before computer use capabilities for engineering workflows
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools

claim: Claude can convert natural language commands to correct bash commands and execute them reliably, including complex operations like database queries and file parsing
actionable: Use natural language to describe bash tasks to Claude; it will translate to correct commands reliably across file operations, data processing, and database interactions
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools

claim: Logging and observability are critical when using bash and file tools with agents; always inspect tool call transcripts to verify what the agent actually did
actionable: Maintain detailed logs of all bash commands and file operations; regularly inspect these logs to verify agent behavior and understand failures
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools

claim: Claude can read files, modify SQL databases, and execute complex multi-step bash operations within a single agentic loop with proper feedback
actionable: Structure agent workflows as: prompt → tool call → execute → get response → re-prompt, allowing agents to perform database updates and file manipulations iteratively
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools

claim: Text-to-action prompts (prompts that result in bash/file operations) are different from text-to-text prompts; they enable agents to control and manipulate actual systems
actionable: Design prompts with the assumption that agents will execute bash commands and modify files, not just generate text responses
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools

claim: Claude 3.5 Opus was removed from Anthropic's documentation; likely because it's not ready yet or Anthropic is refocusing on single powerful models
actionable: Be aware that model availability changes; check official docs rather than relying on memory about which models are available
source_title: Anthropic Computer Use. Where is Claude 3.5 Opus? Sonnet File and Bash Tools
